title: Withania subtriflora
taxonomy: Withania subtriflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae. This plant is closely related to other members of the Solanaceae family, such as tomatoes, potatoes, and eggplants. The classification of Withania subtriflora is further divided into the genus Withania and the kingdom Plantae. The specific classification includes the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Solanales, family Solanaceae, genus Withania, and species W. subtriflora. As a member of the Solanaceae family, Withania subtriflora shares characteristics with other plants in this family.

intro: Withania subtriflora, also known as Dwarf Winter Cherry or Dwarf Withania, is a perennial shrub native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Solanaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Withania subtriflora include alkaloids, withanolides, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
